slides.kicker: // sample template · UX research and product design
slides.lead: Demo presentation for this template. All copy is sample (dummy) text and only exists to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Put the event name, a one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom here. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.
Team slide. Uses initials in a colored circle instead of photos, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: What users do, what we assume and the biggest gaps.
slides.notes: Section divider between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Support tickets, session replays and reviews hold more signal than anyone reads. Value appears when we connect it.
slides.footnote: // illustration slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a punchline or a chapter.

items: higher task success after the checkout redesign
saved per user on first-run setup
bullets: Measure outcomes, not output: impact on users is what counts.
Start where you already have signal and where the pain is sharpest.
A small study with a clear metric beats a big plan without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a big number and a caption, plus supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: You watch real people use the thing before you ship.
bullets: Five users who match the target segment.
Give them a real goal, not a tour.
Where they pause, slip and recover.
The top friction before the next round.
slides.footnote: // two column slide with an illustration
slides.notes: Two column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself.

slides.lead: Same feature, two approaches and two risk curves.
cards: We build on a hunch and hope it lands. Cheap to start, costly to be wrong.
We ask at scale but only learn what we thought to ask.
We talk to a few users in depth and hear the why.
We watch them use it and see where it breaks.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for a comparison or a list of options.

slides.lead: Six common building blocks of a research practice.
cards: Deep one-on-one talks to hear motivations.
Quantify attitudes across a wide group.
See what users actually do at scale.
Learn how users group and label things.
Capture behavior over days, not minutes.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of methods or capabilities.

items: Research runs ad hoc, one study per launch when asked.
Researchers sit in squads and shape decisions early.
Teams run their own studies on a shared playbook.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: You are not the user, and the room full of opinions is not the user either. Go watch one.
slides.by: Sample quote to show the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget under deadline.
bullets: Behavior beats opinion: watch what they do.
Leading questions only confirm your bias.
slides.notes: A plain slide with bullets and a strong lead word. Text scales automatically to fill the space.

slides.lead: A study without a decision attached just gathers dust.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one big sentence centered on the page. Use it for the key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opens the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch to the prototype and watch a real task live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title slide. Contact and a call to action.
